text:
Once upon a time, there was a brave little bird named Rufus. Rufus didn't mind the cold winter nights too much. He had a brilliant white breast and red cheeks perfect for spotting food. His favorite place to sing was the frosty branches of an old maple tree in his yard.

One frosty morning, Rufus saw something he'd never seen before. It was a group of little sprites dancing in the soft light, swirling shapes of sugar dust. They were laughing and whistling, their voices like tinkling bells. The beautiful sound seemed to come from nowhere, just emanating from the air itself.

Curious, Rufus waggled his wings and fluttered closer, but the sprites paid him no attention. They were busy with their whimsical ballet. Rufus decided that he wanted to join their dance. So he hopped closer, shook his head back and forth, and soon found himself joining their merry twirls.
